dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8680 IH: Armed Forces Carry Rights Protection Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Crank introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To amend the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2016 to establish a rebuttable presumption in favor of authorizing a member of the Armed Forces to carry a personal firearm on a military installation.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Armed Forces Carry Rights Protection Act of 2026.

section.header: Rebuttable presumption in favor of authorizing a member of the Armed Forces to carry a personal firearm on a military installation
section.text: Section 526 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2016 (Public Law 114–92; 10 U.S.C. 2672 note) is amended—

paragraph.text: by inserting (a) Establishment.— before Not later; and
by adding at the end the following new subsections:

subsection.text: In the process under subsection (a), there shall be a rebuttable presumption in favor of authorizing a member to carry a firearm owned by such member while the member—

paragraph.text: is on the installation, center, or facility; and
is not performing duty at such installation, center, or facility.

subsection.text: A denial of authorization pursuant to such process shall—

paragraph.text: include an objective, clearly describable, and individualized basis for such denial.
